diagnosis: Two distinct issues found across 8 partial-F1 Elsevier rows. (1) PARSER BUG on 0370-2693(71)90623-x and b978-0-12-811407-0.00020-9: _parse_modern_author_group used self.soup.find('div', class_='author-group') which returns only the first match. Older Phys Lett B reprints and Reference Module book chapters wrap EACH author in their own sibling <div class='author-group'> div, so the parser collected only the first author (Alder, Silva) and missed the rest (Winther; Redondo, Dillard). Confirmed by HTML inspection: surname spans 'Alder','Winther' and 'Fernando Silva','Redondo','Dillard' each in separate author-group siblings. (2) GOLD-QUALITY on remaining 6 rows: gold authors reference a different paper than the cached HTML (j.jat.2015.02.005 gold=Szwarc, HTML=Lubinsky; j.aej.2017.04.016 gold=Abdelgawad/Antoniou/Abdulhai, HTML=Abdel-Aal; j.eats.2017.03.016 gold=Locks,Ryan/1 author, HTML=6 authors led by Renato Locks; j.jspi/j.ijsolstr/j.amc gold has 4-5 authors but HTML surname spans only show the trailing 2). Parser fix: replace find() with find_all() and iterate, preserving existing dedupe.
